commit.message:
fix(security): upgrade axios to 1.15.0 - CVE-2026-40175, CVE-2025-62718 (#966)

Critical S360 action items for SFI-ES5.2 1ES Open Source Vulnerabilities.

CVE-2026-40175 (CVSS 9.9): Unrestricted Cloud Metadata Exfiltration
via Header Injection Chain — prototype pollution gadget enables CRLF
injection in HTTP headers, bypassing AWS IMDSv2 session tokens.

CVE-2025-62718: NO_PROXY Bypass via Hostname Normalization — trailing
dots and IPv6 literals skip NO_PROXY matching, enabling SSRF through
attacker-controlled proxy.

Upgraded in 3 packages:
- extensions/copilot: 1.14.0 → 1.15.0
- extensions/cursor:  1.13.5 → 1.15.0
- agent-os-vscode:    1.13.6 → 1.15.0

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

uninit_dual/uninit_fcodual + NoPullback patterns for these two
foreigncall names that return non-differentiable values.

commit.message:
fix: bound large file primitive hashing

Keep full-file SHA-256 for normal files, but switch oversized file primitive hashing to a bounded prefix SHA-256 with explicit metadata describing the hashing mode and bytes hashed.

Reuse the same guardrail for file-backed content digest fallback and add oversized-file tests for both metadata collection and digest behavior.

commit.message:
fix: use track-local format for PCM padding, add reconnect jitter

- Frame padding now uses the AudioFormat captured at stream start
  (prebuffer.output_format or a snapshot of _normalized_params)
  instead of self._normalized_format which may change mid-stream.
- Reconnect backoff now includes ±20% random jitter to prevent
  thundering-herd reconnects on outages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add stable-named download aliases to releases

The publish job now creates version-less copies of installers
(e.g. HiveMind-aarch64-signed.pkg) alongside the versioned originals.
This lets docs link to /releases/latest/download/HiveMind-aarch64-signed.pkg
which always resolves to the newest release without manual URL updates.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: model switch doubling context usage (#44)

* fix: read only active model's usage to prevent double-counting

The SDK's modelUsage in result messages contains cumulative totals
per model across the entire session. When switching models mid-session
(e.g. opus → haiku), summing all entries double-counted tokens.

Fix: look up only the active model's entry in modelUsage instead of
iterating all values. Falls back to the [1m] suffixed key or the
last entry if the exact key isn't found.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: rewrite README with plugin setup guides and updated architecture

- Add setup instructions for all built-in plugins (GitHub, MemPalace,
  Playwright, Brave Search) with config examples and requirements
- Update architecture diagram to reflect modular backend, model registry,
  background agents, and current file structure
- Update feature descriptions: dynamic model discovery, extended thinking,
  plugin hot-reload, heartbeat/dream agents
- Fix outdated numbers (TypeScript 6.0, 1300+ tests)
- Add custom plugin authoring section with hot-reload note
- Restructure for better scannability

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: clean up features section as a table

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: pass exact SDK model string to processResultMessage

Use options.model (the full SDK model ID including [1m] suffix) for the
modelUsage lookup instead of re-guessing the suffix. Eliminates
duplicated suffix logic between options.ts and stream.ts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update README.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix schedule analysis: exclude surrogate matches from calculations

count toward rankings so they're excluded from favorability and expected

Add git entries for libarchive and boost in cgmanifest.json (#40155)

The OSPO notice@0 task cannot resolve licenses for components
registered with type 'other' (source tarballs). Add parallel
type 'git' entries pointing at the GitHub repos with the exact
commit SHAs for the release tags (v3.7.7 and boost-1.90.0).

The existing 'other' entries are kept for accurate provenance
tracking of the actual tarball downloads used by CMake
FetchContent. The 'git' entries enable the notice generator to
look up licenses from ClearlyDefined.

Co-authored-by: Ben Hillis <benhill@ntdev.microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Improve teams-bot-infra skill: optional endpoint, auto .env, and error handling (#80)

* Fix Azure CLI detection on Windows using cmd /c wrapper

Root cause: execFile() cannot spawn .cmd files directly on Windows,
resulting in spawn EINVAL error. Previous attempts used shell:true
which introduced command injection vulnerabilities.

Solution: Use cmd.exe with /c flag to execute az.cmd on Windows,
which is secure (blocks injection) and cross-platform compatible.

- Windows: cmd /c az.cmd <args>
- Unix: az <args>

Security: Verified that special characters (&&, ||, ;) are treated
as literal arguments, not executed as shell commands.

Fixes #28

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add teams-bot-infra agent skill for Teams bot infrastructure management

Implement production-grade agent skill following the 5-phase development lifecycle:
- Phase 1: Discovered and validated workflow via manual execution
- Phase 2: Designed modular architecture (single skill covering create, configure, troubleshoot)
- Phase 3: Implemented SKILL.md with verification checkpoints and error recovery
- Phase 4: Validated against real workflow (leveraged Phase 1 testing)
- Phase 5: Packaged for distribution with installation documentation

Skill features:
- Complete bot creation workflow (CLI install → auth → create → verify)
- Verification checkpoints requiring evidence at each step
- Common operations (update endpoint, view apps, update CLI)
- Error recovery (auth required, sideload disabled)
- Resource links (Teams SDK, devtunnels, Microsoft docs)

Scope: Bot infrastructure only (excludes bot code development and hosting)

Installation: npx skills add heyitsaamir/teamscli --skill teams-bot-infra

Resolves #37

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: improve wording in teams-bot-infra skill description

Change "guides you through" to "will guide you in" for better clarity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Improve teams-bot-infra skill: optional endpoint, auto .env, and error handling

- Make messaging endpoint optional for proactive-only bots
  - Add proactive flows explanation and warning
  - Note that endpoint can be added later via teams app edit
- Change credential handling to auto-write to .env file by default
  - Prompt user for existing .env or create new one
  - Safely append/update without overwriting other values
- Add "This app cannot be found" error recovery
  - Explain cross-tenant installation issue
  - Common scenario: M365 Developer Program vs work tenant

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: prefer system python for workspace runtime

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: improve mobile layout on recipe edit, new, and detail pages

- Reduce outer container padding from p-8 to p-4 sm:p-8 on all three pages
- Ingredient row: shrink qty (w-24→w-14 sm:w-24) and unit (w-32→w-20 sm:w-32)
  inputs so ingredient name field has room on narrow viewports
- Ingredients/Instructions section headers: stack action buttons below title
  on mobile using flex-col sm:flex-row with flex-wrap on button group
- Section group add-ingredient/add-step buttons: hide verbose label text on
  mobile (hidden sm:inline), showing icon only to prevent overflow
- New recipe page: stack 'Import from URL' button below title on mobile
- Add recipe_new page and dynamic recipe_edit capture to screenshot script

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: compute mutation score from counts instead of r.mutationScore (#605)

* fix: compute mutation score from counts instead of r.mutationScore

The top-level mutationScore field in Stryker's JSON report was null/undefined,
causing the score to display as 0.0% even when 444/1415 mutations were killed
(~31%). Compute the score from the per-mutant counts already read in the loop.

Score formula: killed / (killed + survived + timedOut) * 100

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: expand mutation testing to all files + add concurrency benchmark

- stryker.config.mjs: add usageAnalysis.js, maturityScoring.js,
  workspaceHelpers.js, claudecode.js, and all backend/** files to
  mutate list. Wire in all 40 unit test files (skip viewRegression
  which requires JSDOM). Use Stryker micromatch globs for backend/.
- mutation-benchmark.yml: new workflow_dispatch workflow that runs
  Stryker with concurrency [2,4,8,16] in a matrix. Each variant
  reports wall-clock time + mutation score to the step summary and
  uploads an artifact. Use to find the optimal concurrency setting.
- ci.yml: bump mutation test timeout from 20 to 60 minutes to
  accommodate the larger mutate scope.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger mutation benchmark on changes to its own workflow file

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: add --test-bail to Stryker command runner for early mutant kill

When a mutant is killed, Node.js exits immediately instead of running
all remaining tests. This can reduce per-mutant test time from
'all tests' to 'first killing test', which should cut overall runtime
significantly especially for well-tested code.

Note: the expanded mutate scope (backend/**) pushed mutant count from
~1,420 to ~12,907 — if runtime is still too high after this fix we
should scope the backend glob back down.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: also trigger mutation benchmark on stryker.config.mjs changes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove --test-bail (not in Node 24), use coverageAnalysis: 'all'

--test-bail is not a valid flag in Node.js 24.14.1 and causes Stryker's
dry run to fail immediately.

Replace with coverageAnalysis: 'all': Stryker instruments the source
files in a dry run to discover which mutants are actually covered by the
test suite. Any mutant with zero coverage is skipped without running
tests, reducing the overall test run count significantly — especially
for the backend/** files that may have incomplete test coverage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: update benchmark matrix to [16, 32, 64]

2/4/8 are too slow (~7h remaining at 16 concurrency). Test higher
concurrency to see if more parallelism helps on the ubuntu runner.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: replace inPlace with explicit files to eliminate worker race conditions

inPlace: true causes all concurrent workers to share the same out/src/
directory. When concurrency > 1 and two workers need to mutate the same
file simultaneously, they corrupt each other's mutations — producing
wrong kills, wrong survivals, and spurious timeouts.

Fix: remove inPlace: true and add a files array that explicitly includes
out/** (which is gitignored and excluded from sandboxes by default).
Stryker now creates one isolated sandbox copy per worker. node_modules
is always symlinked automatically, so per-worker disk overhead is small.

The old workaround note ('run compile-tests to restore mutated files')
is no longer needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: drop backend/** and large files from mutate scope

backend/** (syncService 1430 lines, facade 1136 lines etc.) generates
~10k+ mutants with low kill rates, blowing the CI time budget at any
realistic concurrency. usageAnalysis (~1900 lines) and maturityScoring
(~1200 lines) have the same problem.

Keeping: tokenEstimation, sessionParser, workspaceHelpers, claudecode,
and all utils/* — these are well-scoped and well-tested.

Also update benchmark matrix to [8, 16, 32] now that isolated sandboxes
make higher concurrency meaningful for the reduced scope.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: revert sandbox and coverageAnalysis changes, back to inPlace

Both changes introduced since the working 7-minute run caused all jobs
to fail:

1. files: ['out/**'] sandbox — copying out/** per worker is too slow
   and disk-heavy on 2-vCPU ubuntu runners. At 8/16/32 concurrency
   this caused timeouts before Stryker even started testing.

2. coverageAnalysis: 'all' — does not flush coverage data correctly
   through --test-force-exit, causing dry run hangs or bad results.

Revert both to the known-working state (inPlace: true, off).
Keep all the good changes: expanded test file list (40 tests),
reduced mutate scope (7 files, no backend/large files).

Benchmark matrix updated to [4, 8, 16] — concurrency 4 was confirmed
working at 7 min before, these bracket the likely sweet spot.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: bump benchmark step timeout from 60 to 120 minutes

16x concurrency is projecting ~1h 29m for 2735 mutants. 4x and 8x
will be even slower. Give all variants room to complete.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: trim test runner to only files that test the mutated source files

Running 40 test files per mutant caused every mutant to approach the
timeoutMS (15s), producing a near-zero kill rate (~0.1%). The 31 backend
test files don't import from any of the 7 mutated source files so they
add no killing power — only overhead.

Revert to 10 focused test files (original 9 + claudecode.test.js) that
directly exercise the mutated code. This restores the ~31% kill rate
seen in the original working run.

Rule: only include test files in the Stryker command runner if they
actually import from one of the files listed in mutate.

Also reduce benchmark matrix to [4, 8] until baseline is confirmed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: add runner dimension to benchmark matrix (ubuntu vs windows)

Matrix is now 2x2: concurrency [4, 8] x runner [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest].
Each variant uploads its own artifact named by runner+concurrency.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add improve-mutation-score agent

Downloads the latest mutation-report artifact (or runs Stryker locally),
parses surviving mutants, maps them back to TypeScript source, triages
which are worth killing vs equivalent/trivial, and adds focused tests.

Includes guardrails to avoid chasing the score with empty assertions or
mocked-out tests that kill mutants without providing real coverage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: trim benchmark matrix to ubuntu-latest concurrency=4 only

- Commented out concurrency=8 on ubuntu: same throughput as 4, no benefit
- Commented out all windows-latest entries: significantly slower runners
  for this workload (observed in CI runs)

All commented entries are kept for easy re-enabling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(calls): fix creator_user_id HMAC to use string format in invite-service

creator_user_id was derived from invite.uuid which has type Uuid.
Calling .as_bytes() on Uuid returns 16-byte binary, but signaling-service
computes HMACs from the string form (36-byte UUID string) received via
x-user-id header.

The previous fix only corrected accepter_user_id. This fixes creator_user_id
so both sides of the contact_link are stored with the same HMAC format that
signaling-service uses in are_mutual_contacts().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add configurable output sample rate and bit depth

Add two new config entries:
- Output sample rate: Auto/44100/48000/96000 Hz
- Output bit depth: Auto/16-bit/24-bit

'Auto' (default) preserves current behavior — format is detected
from YM quality setting. Explicit values override auto-detection,
allowing users to force Hi-Res (24/96) or CD quality (16/44.1).

commit.message:
fix: statically link lzma to avoid codesign Team ID mismatch

hive-daemon was dynamically linking to Homebrew's liblzma.5.dylib via
the xz2 crate. After codesigning, macOS dyld rejects the library due
to mismatched Team IDs, causing the daemon to crash-loop on launch.

Adding lzma-sys with the 'static' feature compiles lzma from source
and embeds it in the binary, eliminating the runtime dependency.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ui): Sprint 32B — header-tabs overlap, chip ring selection, token consistency

- Fix IO rootMargin -32px → -52px to match --height-header token
Tier 3:

commit.message:
[WIP] Fix httpx AsyncClient resource leak in BotApplication (#81)

* Initial plan

* fix: close httpx AsyncClient to prevent resource leak

- Add aclose() method to ConversationClient to close underlying HTTP client
- Add aclose() and async context manager support to BotApplication
- Update BotApp (FastAPI) to use lifespan hook for automatic cleanup
- Add tests for resource cleanup and context manager support
- Update FastAPI sample to demonstrate proper cleanup pattern

Fixes resource leak where httpx AsyncClient was never closed, causing
TCP connections, file descriptors, and memory to not be released in
long-running servers.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/rido-min/botas/sessions/6d7186cc-dded-4734-b8c1-be840de5dfbc

Co-authored-by: rido-min <14916339+rido-min@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve ruff import sort lint issue in bot_app.py

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/rido-min/botas/sessions/486f1d88-b0d8-4999-bc49-18c9ee04efbd

Co-authored-by: rido-min <14916339+rido-min@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add typing activity support (sendTyping) across all languages (#82)

* chore: log codebase audit session

- Orchestration logs for amy-audit (.NET), fry-audit (Node.js), hermes-audit (Python)
- Session log for 2026-04-13T0805 audit batch (timestamp ISO 8601 UTC)
- Cross-agent history updates append audit results to each agent's memory
- 3 critical findings identified across implementations (exception typo, JSON security, resource leak)
- Ready for remediation sprint

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove OnTyping/onTyping handler registration from documentation

- Clarify that typing handler registration uses standard on('typing', handler) pattern
- Remove OnTyping() dedicated method references from specs/ActivityPayloads.md
- Remove on_typing() method references from docs-site/typing-activity.md
- Remove typing handler registration row from specs/README.md language differences table
- Keep sendTyping()/SendTypingAsync()/send_typing() convenience methods intact
- Main README.md had no OnTyping references to remove

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add typing activity support (sendTyping) across all languages

- Add SendTypingAsync() to TurnContext (.NET) returning Task<string>
- Add sendTyping() to TurnContext (Node.js) returning Promise<void>
- Add send_typing() to TurnContext (Python) returning None
- Add typing activity spec to ActivityPayloads.md
- Add docs-site typing activity guide
- Update README with typing examples
- Tests: .NET 50 pass, Node 69 pass, Python 70 pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: rido <rido@no-mail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: simplify Net Flow chart to single bar per week

Replace grouped arrivals/completions bars + secondary-axis line with
a single bar per week showing net = completions - arrivals.
- Green bar when net >= 0 (shipping faster than work arrives)
- Red bar when net < 0 (backlog growing)
- Dashed purple trendline on the same axis
- Y-axis label clarifies the formula: 'done − started'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace delete with archive in UI, add archive toggle in Historial

commit.message:
fix(fabric): use import instead of builtins for notebookutils detection (#157)

notebookutils is a regular site-package on the Fabric runtime, not a
builtins attribute. The previous getattr(builtins, 'notebookutils')
approach failed silently, causing Fabric environment detection to
always return False.

Also treat OPENAI_API_KEY='place_holder_for_fabric_internal' as absent
so the library correctly falls through to Azure / Entra ID auth.

Co-authored-by: Hiroki Mizukami <hmizukami+microsoft@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: make source risk sorting explicit

Replace the custom sorted_by iterator extension with an explicit local Vec::sort_by flow in source analysis so the risky-file ordering logic is obvious and dependency-safe.

Add a regression test that preserves the count-then-path ordering semantics for top_risky_files.
